talks.title: Civil Work: Putting Syrian refugees back on their feet
talks.speaker: Rasha Maaty
talks.speakerBio: Rasha Maaty is a social impact advocate focused on humanitarian work and refugee support. Her work centers on creating sustainable livelihoods and integration programs for displaced communities.

talks.title: Restructuring our living spaces through container architecture
talks.speaker: Youssef Farag
talks.speakerBio: Youssef Farag is an architect and innovator exploring sustainable and affordable housing solutions through creative architectural design. His work utilizes recycled and unconventional materials to address housing challenges.

talks.title: Can we still say that there are plenty of fish in the sea by 2050?
talks.speaker: Nada El Shanawany
talks.speakerBio: Nada El Shanawany is a marine biologist and environmental scientist dedicated to studying and protecting marine ecosystems. Her research addresses the critical challenges facing ocean sustainability and biodiversity.

talks.title: Drama Therapy: An unlikely solution to a common problem
talks.speaker: Reem Hashem
talks.speakerBio: Reem Hashem is a drama therapist and mental health professional who uses theatrical techniques and creative expression as therapeutic tools. She pioneered drama therapy programs addressing psychological and emotional challenges in diverse populations.

talks.title: A biomarker saving lives
talks.speaker: Rasha Hanafi
talks.speakerBio: Rasha Hanafi is a biomedical researcher and healthcare innovator focused on developing diagnostic tools and biomarkers to improve patient outcomes. Her research bridges the gap between laboratory discovery and clinical application.

talks.title: Mental illness representation in Egyptian movies
talks.speaker: Caroline Mitry
talks.speakerBio: Caroline Mitry is a media studies scholar and cultural critic examining the portrayal of mental health issues in film and television. Her work advocates for more accurate and stigma-reducing representation of mental illness in media.

talks.title: A sustainable development model for red sea rural areas
talks.speaker: Omar Sabaa
talks.speakerBio: Omar Sabaa is a development expert and environmental consultant specializing in sustainable community development in rural and coastal regions. His work integrates economic growth with environmental conservation and social welfare.

talks.title: Post chemotherapy: Getting ovaries back to life
talks.speaker: Sara Abdel-Aziz
talks.speakerBio: Sara Abdel-Aziz is a reproductive health specialist and oncology advocate focused on fertility preservation and quality of life for cancer survivors. Her work addresses the overlooked intersection of cancer treatment and reproductive health.

talks.title: Research: The marginalized dimension in our fight against cancer
talks.speaker: Yehia Abugabal
talks.speakerBio: Yehia Abugabal is a cancer researcher and public health advocate emphasizing the importance of diverse and inclusive research in improving cancer treatment outcomes. His work highlights underrepresented populations in medical research.

talks.title: Beyond limits, different is beautiful
talks.speaker: Rania Abdelaziz & Alaa El Masry
talks.speakerBio: Rania Abdelaziz and Alaa El Masry are advocates for inclusion and accessibility. Their work focuses on challenging perceptions about disability and celebrating human diversity.

talks.title: Reclaiming Egypt's narrative
talks.speaker: Mahmoud Hawary
talks.speakerBio: Mahmoud Hawary is a cultural historian and filmmaker dedicated to preserving and promoting Egypt's rich historical narratives.

talks.title: From machines to bodies
talks.speaker: Yahya Hefnawi
talks.speakerBio: Yahya Hefnawi is an engineer and innovator exploring the intersection of technology and human physiology.

talks.title: Getting companies racing to hire you
talks.speaker: Youssef El Akkari
talks.speakerBio: Youssef El Akkari is a career coach and talent development specialist helping young professionals stand out.

talks.title: Bridging the professional gap: The transformative power of network
talks.speaker: Ahmed El Helaly
talks.speakerBio: Ahmed El Helaly is a professional development coach emphasizing the transformative impact of strategic professional relationships.

talks.title: It didn't start with you
talks.speaker: Shereen Rizk
talks.speakerBio: Shereen Rizk is a psychotherapist exploring generational trauma and healing.

talks.title: Creating movie magic!
talks.speaker: Dibo
talks.speakerBio: Dibo is a filmmaker and visual effects artist passionate about bringing stories to life.

talks.title: Bringing the past to life
talks.speaker: Rowan El Selmy
talks.speakerBio: Rowan El Selmy is an archaeologist dedicated to uncovering and preserving historical narratives.

talks.title: Goal Of Equality
talks.speaker: Ali Aboulnasr
talks.speakerBio: Ali Aboulnasr is a social justice advocate focused on equality and civic impact.

talks.title: STEM-ED
talks.speaker: Mohamed Rizkallah
talks.speakerBio: Mohamed Rizkallah is an education and technology speaker who explores the intersection of STEM and learning.

talks.title: Exposing hidden dimensions of Child Sexual Abuse.
talks.speaker: Sara Aziz
talks.speakerBio: Sara Aziz is a health advocate raising awareness about child safety and support systems.

talks.title: Criminals in Suits
talks.speaker: Sue Ellen Hassouna
talks.speakerBio: Sue Ellen Hassouna is a speaker exploring ethics, justice, and the hidden realities behind appearances.

talks.title: Art therapy: a world beyond creative expression
talks.speaker: Carol Hammal
talks.speakerBio: Carol Hammal is an art therapy advocate who uses creativity as a path to healing and expression.

2025.title: Chairwoman
2025.name: Sama El-Badrawy
2025.description: The Chairman oversees the entire TEDxGUC operation, ensuring the event aligns with TED's mission of spreading ideas worth sharing.
2025.committeeDescription: The Chairman's office sets the strategic direction for the entire TEDxGUC organization. Responsible for external partnerships, TED licensing compliance, and ensuring every division works in harmony toward delivering a world-class event.
2025.memberBio: Sama is a business administration graduate and visionary leader passionate about creating platforms for transformative ideas. With experience in event management and community building, she brings strategic thinking and collaborative spirit to the role.

children.title: Curator — Talks
children.name: Yehia Amr
children.description: Leads the Talks division, curating speakers and ensuring content quality aligns with the event's theme.
children.committeeDescription: The Talks division is the heart of TEDxGUC. This team scouts, selects, and develops speakers — ensuring every talk is compelling, well-researched, and delivered with impact.
children.memberBio: Yehia is an engineering student with a deep passion for innovative storytelling and public discourse. He combines his analytical mindset with creative vision to identify speakers who challenge conventions and inspire audiences.

children.title: Coaching
children.name: Mariam Adel
children.description: Coaches speakers through rehearsals and presentation skills workshops.
children.committeeDescription: The Coaching committee works one-on-one with speakers to refine their narratives, improve delivery, and ensure every talk resonates with the audience.
children.memberBio: Mariam is a psychology student with experience in public speaking coaching and debate.

children.title: Research & Production
children.name: Hassan Tarek
children.description: Conducts research to support speakers and manages the production pipeline.
children.committeeDescription: Research & Production ensures every talk is backed by solid research and produced to the highest standards — from script development to stage design.
children.memberBio: Hassan is a political science student with a keen eye for detail and a love for investigative research.

children.title: Curator — Operations
children.name: Kareem Shawish
children.description: Manages all operational logistics, event planning, and day-of execution.
children.committeeDescription: Operations is the backbone of TEDxGUC. From venue logistics to vendor management, this division ensures every detail is accounted for so the event runs flawlessly.
children.memberBio: Kareem is a systems engineer known for his meticulous attention to detail and problem-solving prowess. He thrives under pressure and excels at orchestrating complex operations, ensuring every element aligns perfectly for a seamless event experience.

children.title: Events Management
children.name: Dina Sherif
children.description: Plans and executes all event activities.
children.committeeDescription: Events Management handles venue coordination, scheduling, and on-site attendee experience from doors-open to closing remarks.
children.memberBio: Dina is an architecture student who brings her spatial awareness and creativity to event design.

children.title: Partnerships
children.name: Ali Mahmoud
children.description: Builds relationships with sponsors and stakeholders.
children.committeeDescription: Partnerships secures sponsorships and strategic alliances that fund and elevate the TEDxGUC experience.
children.memberBio: Ali is a finance student with strong networking skills and a talent for negotiation.

children.title: Logistics
children.name: Rana Hossam
children.description: Handles transportation, equipment, catering, and venue setup.
children.committeeDescription: Logistics manages the physical infrastructure of the event — from equipment rental and catering to transportation and setup.
children.memberBio: Rana is an industrial engineering student who thrives on solving complex logistical puzzles.

children.title: Curator — OD
children.name: Hazem Talhawy
children.description: Focuses on team growth, internal culture, and organizational excellence.
children.committeeDescription: Organizational Development nurtures the TEDxGUC team itself — running training programs, building culture, and ensuring every member grows alongside the organization.
children.memberBio: Hazem is an organizational psychology specialist dedicated to building high-performing teams and fostering inclusive cultures. He believes that a thriving organization starts with investing in people and creating spaces where everyone can contribute their best.

children.title: Associate Development
children.name: Youssef Gamal
children.description: Develops training programs for new associates.
children.committeeDescription: Associate Development creates onboarding programs, workshops, and mentorship tracks for new team members.
children.memberBio: Youssef is an HR management student who believes in investing in people as the key to organizational success.

children.title: Associate Experience
children.name: Farida Nasser
children.description: Ensures a positive experience for all team members.
children.committeeDescription: Associate Experience designs team-bonding activities, feedback systems, and wellness initiatives to keep morale high.
children.memberBio: Farida is a psychology student dedicated to creating inclusive and supportive team environments.

children.title: Curator — Marketing
children.name: Jana Nader
children.description: Directs all marketing, branding, and communication strategies.
children.committeeDescription: Marketing & Coordination amplifies the TEDxGUC brand through social media, content creation, and integrated campaigns that drive engagement and ticket sales.
children.memberBio: Jana is a creative marketing strategist with a flair for storytelling and data-driven decision making. She combines bold creative vision with analytical rigor to craft campaigns that resonate with diverse audiences and drive meaningful engagement.

children.title: Media & Design
children.name: Nada Emad
children.description: Creates all visual content and social media assets.
children.committeeDescription: Media & Design produces the visual identity of TEDxGUC — from social posts and event branding to video content and stage graphics.
children.memberBio: Nada is a digital media student with a portfolio spanning photography, motion graphics, and UI design.

children.title: Campaigns & Copy
children.name: Ziad Ayman
children.description: Develops marketing campaigns and writes compelling copy.
children.committeeDescription: Campaigns & Copywriting crafts the voice of TEDxGUC — writing copy, planning content calendars, and executing multi-channel marketing campaigns.
children.memberBio: Ziad is a journalism student who combines data-driven strategy with creative storytelling.

2026.title: Chairman
2026.name: Kareem Shawish
2026.description: Leading TEDxGUC into 2026 with a renewed vision for cross-disciplinary dialogue and community impact.
2026.committeeDescription: The Chairman's office sets the strategic direction for the entire TEDxGUC organization, overseeing all divisions and maintaining TED licensing standards.
2026.memberBio: Kareem is an accomplished operations strategist with a track record of scaling events and organizations to greater impact. His leadership philosophy combines operational excellence with empathy, creating environments where innovation thrives and teams feel valued and empowered.

children.title: Curator — Talks
children.name: Moaz Akram
children.description: Curating a diverse lineup of speakers who challenge conventional thinking.
children.committeeDescription: The Talks division scouts, selects, and develops speakers to deliver compelling, well-researched talks that align with the season's theme.
children.memberBio: Moaz is a passionate curator with a keen ear for compelling narratives and transformative ideas. He believes every story has power, and his mission is to discover speakers whose unique perspectives can shift how people see the world and their place in it.

children.title: Coaching
children.name: Ammar Ibrahim
children.description: Guiding speakers through intensive coaching.
children.committeeDescription: Coaching works closely with each speaker to refine their narrative arc, improve delivery, and build confidence for the stage.
children.memberBio: Ammar is a communication coach and performance strategist who has worked with hundreds of speakers across diverse fields. He excels at identifying a speaker's authentic voice and helping them communicate their ideas with clarity, passion, and impact.

children.title: Research & Production
children.name: Abdulrahman Waleed
children.description: Researching cutting-edge topics and managing production.
children.committeeDescription: Research & Production ensures every talk is backed by credible sources and produced with professional-grade quality.
children.memberBio: Abdulrahman is a meticulous researcher and producer who brings talks to life through rigorous research and creative production direction. His commitment to authenticity and quality ensures that every idea presented on stage is both intellectually sound and emotionally compelling.

children.title: Curator — Operations
children.name: Kareem Roshdy
children.description: Ensuring flawless event execution from planning to post-event wrap-up.
children.committeeDescription: Operations coordinates all logistical elements of the event — from venue booking and vendor contracts to day-of execution and post-event teardown.
children.memberBio: Kareem is a logistics expert with an impressive portfolio of large-scale events executed flawlessly. He combines strategic planning with hands-on leadership, ensuring every operational detail supports the overall vision while remaining flexible to adapt to unexpected challenges.

children.title: Events Management
children.name: Laila El-Hadidy
children.description: Coordinating all event logistics and attendee experience.
children.committeeDescription: Events Management designs the full attendee journey from registration to closing, including interactive experiences and networking sessions.
children.memberBio: Laila is an experiential event designer who creates memorable moments that leave lasting impressions. She approaches every event detail as an opportunity to delight attendees and foster meaningful connections among participants.

children.title: Partnerships
children.name: Hamza El-Khobby
children.description: Securing strategic partnerships and sponsor relations.
children.committeeDescription: Partnerships builds and maintains relationships with corporate sponsors, media partners, and community organizations.
children.memberBio: Hamza is a relationship builder with strong business acumen and diplomatic skills. He understands that great partnerships are built on mutual value and trust, and he works diligently to create win-win collaborations that elevate TEDxGUC and benefit all stakeholders.

children.title: Logistics
children.name: Abdallah Nazih
children.description: Managing all on-ground logistics and vendor coordination.
children.committeeDescription: Logistics handles the physical infrastructure — equipment, catering, transportation, signage, and venue preparation.
children.memberBio: Abdallah is a logistics coordinator with a natural talent for solving complex supply chain puzzles. His meticulous planning and vendor management ensure that all physical elements of the event are perfectly coordinated to create a seamless attendee experience.

children.title: Curator — OD
children.name: Nour Ayman
children.description: Building a stronger, more connected team culture.
children.committeeDescription: Organizational Development focuses on internal team health — running training workshops, mentorship programs, and culture-building initiatives.
children.memberBio: Nour is an organizational development specialist passionate about creating inclusive, thriving work cultures. She believes that the strength of an organization is directly reflected in the wellbeing and growth of its team members.

children.title: Associate Development
children.name: Rohanda Ahmed
children.description: Creating growth opportunities and mentorship programs.
children.committeeDescription: Associate Development designs onboarding tracks, skill-building workshops, and career mentorship for all new associates.
children.memberBio: Rohanda is a talent development specialist who sees potential in every team member and creates pathways for growth and excellence. Her mentorship programs are known for transforming first-time volunteers into confident leaders.

children.title: Associate Experience
children.name: Abdullah Abosamra
children.description: Designing engaging team experiences and well-being initiatives.
children.committeeDescription: Associate Experience ensures every team member feels valued through social events, recognition programs, and well-being support.
children.memberBio: Abdullah is a well-being advocate and event creator who designs meaningful experiences that strengthen team bonds and celebrate individual contributions. He believes that a happy team is a productive team.

children.title: Curator — Marketing
children.name: Laila Eid
children.description: Amplifying TEDxGUC's reach through innovative marketing strategies.
children.committeeDescription: Marketing & Coordination drives brand awareness, manages social channels, and creates integrated campaigns to engage the TEDxGUC community.
children.memberBio: Laila is a forward-thinking marketing leader with expertise in digital strategy and community engagement. She crafts compelling brand narratives that resonate across platforms and creates campaigns that spark conversation and drive meaningful audience connection.

children.title: Media & Design
children.name: Farida Sabrah
children.description: Producing visual storytelling content across all platforms.
children.committeeDescription: Media & Design creates the visual identity of TEDxGUC — social media graphics, event branding, video production, and stage visuals.
children.memberBio: Farida is a multimedia artist and designer with a gift for translating complex ideas into stunning visual narratives. Her creative work consistently elevates the TEDxGUC brand and captivates audiences across all touchpoints.

children.title: Campaigns & Copy
children.name: Malak Hatem
children.description: Crafting compelling narratives and managing multi-channel campaigns.
children.committeeDescription: Campaigns & Copywriting develops the voice of TEDxGUC — from taglines and social captions to email newsletters and press releases.
children.memberBio: Malak is a talented copywriter and campaign strategist who brings the TEDxGUC message to life through compelling storytelling. Her work balances creative expression with strategic thinking to maximize impact and engagement.

events.recap: Our world operates in a dynamic design of opposite yet complementary forces. TEDxGUC "Back & Forth" explores how life exists in constant motion between opposing yet complementary states.

events.recap: An alloy is a compound mixture or union of distinct parties that exchange characteristics to optimize a desired outcome. TEDxGUC ALLOY explored how diverse elements combine to create stronger solutions.

events.recap: All that glitters is not gold. This event challenged perspectives on appearances and true value, exploring the lessons learned from both shiny surfaces and overlooked treasures.

speakers.talkTitle: Not All That Glitters: Seeing Through Illusions

speakers.name: Youssef Mohsen
speakers.talkTitle: The Hidden Value in Overlooked Things

events.recap: TEDxGUC 'Whitespace' explored hidden spaces where ideas grow. The event gathered speakers across culture, technology, psychology, and business to challenge assumptions and inspire practical action.

events.recap: Beyond the echoes of faint whispers and symphonies of words lies a million untold narratives. TEDxGUC ECHO explores the impact of words and opinions, posing the question: Will we separate the souls, artists and personalities from the opinions or will cancel culture thrive?

speakers.name: Hanaa Medhat
speakers.talkTitle: Words That Matter

speakers.name: Menna Metwally
speakers.talkTitle: The Echo of Our Choices

events.recap: TEDxGUC "Glitch" explored unexpected disruptions in our digital and physical worlds. From technological malfunctions to societal upheavals, glitches can be both destructive and transformative.

events.recap: TEDxGUC "The Pillars of Everything" explored the silent convictions, formative experiences, and moments that hold us together when everything else shifts. In a world obsessed with outcomes, we often overlook what lies beneath.

With over 1000 attendees, the event featured immersive experiences, networking sessions, and thought-provoking talks that challenged conventional thinking.

upcomingEvent.description: Ideas that ignite change. TEDxGUC 'upsidedown' will bring together innovators, artists, and thinkers who spark transformation in their communities.

upcomingSchedule.title: Doors Open & Registration
upcomingSchedule.description: Check-in, grab your badge, and enjoy coffee.

upcomingSchedule.title: Session 1 — Spark
upcomingSchedule.description: Three talks exploring the origins of transformative ideas.

upcomingSchedule.title: Networking Break
upcomingSchedule.description: Connect with fellow attendees over refreshments.

upcomingSchedule.title: Session 2 — Ignite
upcomingSchedule.description: Three talks on turning ideas into action.

upcomingSchedule.title: Lunch Break
upcomingSchedule.description: Catered lunch and interactive experience booths.

upcomingSchedule.title: Session 3 — Transform
upcomingSchedule.description: Three closing talks on lasting impact and legacy.

upcomingSchedule.title: Closing & Networking
upcomingSchedule.description: Closing remarks followed by open networking.

upcomingFAQs.question: What is the dress code?
upcomingFAQs.answer: Smart casual is recommended. Please dress comfortably as the event runs for a full day. Avoid overly casual attire like shorts or flip-flops.

upcomingFAQs.question: Are refunds available?
upcomingFAQs.answer: Tickets are non-refundable but transferable. If you can't attend, you may transfer your ticket to someone else by contacting us at least 48 hours before the event.

upcomingFAQs.question: What is the filming policy?
upcomingFAQs.answer: Professional filming will take place throughout the event. By attending, you consent to being photographed or filmed. Personal recording of talks is not permitted.

upcomingFAQs.question: Is parking available?
upcomingFAQs.answer: Free parking is available at the GUC campus. We recommend arriving early as spaces fill up quickly.

upcomingFAQs.question: Can I bring a guest?
upcomingFAQs.answer: Each ticket admits one person. If you'd like to bring a guest, they will need their own ticket.
